summary: tailwind-animate-v4 typosquats tailwindcss-animate and executes obfuscated payload on require()
details: The package name mimics the legitimate 'tailwindcss-animate' Tailwind CSS plugin. index.js presents a plausible Tailwind plugin body via module.exports, but after a long run of tab whitespace on the same line contains an eval(atob(...)) call that decodes and executes an obfuscated payload whenever the module is required. The decoded first stage is a permutation-based string-cipher deobfuscator that assigns require into the global scope (global[_$_376e[0]] = require) and invokes a second obfuscated body, a shape characteristic of npm loader malware. Whitespace padding is used to hide the eval from casual editor review. A Tailwind CSS plugin has no legitimate need for base64-decoded, eval'd code at load time; the combination of name-confusion with a top ecosystem package, deliberate concealment, and dynamic code execution on import constitutes a typosquat-with-payload that runs attacker-controlled code in the installer's Node.js process on require().

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
